en.extract:
The scarlet-thighed dacnis (Dacnis venusta) is a tanager native to Central and northernmost South America. 


Taxonomy
The species name, venusta, means lovely or beautiful.


Description
The iris of the scarlet-thighed dacnis is bright red in male individuals, and duller red in the females. The males have a characteristic bright turquoise blue crown and nape, sides of head and neck, centre of back, rump and scapulars. The forehead, lores, sides of back, wings and tail, mid-throat, and the entire remaining underparts of the male birds are black; the thighs are scarlet, but these are usually hidden. The females are a dull greenish blue above, which is brightest on their cheeks, scapulars, and rump, and duskier on their back, wings, and tail; below dingy buffy greyish, buffiest on belly and undertail coverts.


Distribution and habitat
The scarlet-thighed tanager is found in Costa Rica, Colombia, Ecuador and Panama.


References


Further reading


External links

The '''scarlet-thighed dacnis''' ('''''Dacnis venusta''''') is a [[tanager]] native to [[Central America|Central]] and northernmost [[South America]]. 

== Taxonomy ==
The species name, ''venusta,'' means lovely or beautiful.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Obando |first=Allan |last2=Richart |first2=Casey H. |last3=Burns |first3=Kevin J. |date=2020 |title=Scarlet-thighed Dacnis (Dacnis venusta), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/sctdac1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.sctdac1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Description==
The iris of the scarlet-thighed dacnis is bright red in male individuals, and duller red in the females. The males have a characteristic bright turquoise blue crown and nape, sides of head and neck, centre of back, rump and [[scapular]]s. The forehead, [[Lore (anatomy)|lores]], sides of back, wings and tail, mid-throat, and the entire remaining underparts of the male birds are black; the thighs are scarlet, but these are usually hidden. The females are a dull greenish blue above, which is brightest on their cheeks, scapulars, and rump, and duskier on their back, wings, and tail; below dingy buffy greyish, buffiest on belly and undertail coverts.<ref name=":0" />

== Distribution and habitat ==
The scarlet-thighed tanager is found in [[Costa Rica]], [[Colombia]], [[Ecuador]] and [[Panama]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dacnis venusta (Scarlet-thighed Dacnis) - Avibase |url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=223EB3AD052CEC00 |access-date=2026-03-13 |website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org}}</ref>[[File:Scarlet-thighed Dacnis.jpg|thumb|left| Altos Del Maria, El Valle - Panama]]
[[File: Scarlet-thighed_Dacnis_f.jpg|thumb|left|female - Selva Verde Lodge - Costa Rica]]
